title: A Light Weight Multi-Distance fNIRS Dataset for Ball-Squeezing Task and Purposeful Motion Artifact Creation Task
description: A high-density functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) dataset acquired from the motor cortex during three experimental paradigms: resting state, ball-squeezing motor tasks, and purposeful motion artifact creation. The dataset includes accelerometer recordings and is designed to support development and validation of motion artifact correction and image reconstruction methods in fNIRS neuroimaging.
methods_description: High-density fNIRS data were collected from the motor cortex using short-separation and long-separation source-detector configurations. Participants performed three experimental tasks: a 5-minute resting state, ball-squeezing tasks (3 runs, 24 seconds each), and a motion artifact creation task involving six instructed movements (head tilting right/left/up/down, eyebrow raising, and vocalization). Accelerometer data were recorded as auxiliary measurements.
